issues.objection: A cynical writer's mocking gloss of 7.21 would target either (a) 'just commit to your stack, bro' or (b) 'work harder, but spiritual.' The engineering layer forecloses both: the falsifiability field explicitly rejects the soft reading; the counter_example carves out where the verse does not apply. The quotable_line ("Whatever stack the engineer commits to with disciplined attention, the substrate of that stack rewards the commitment.…") is concrete enough to resist parody. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims a personal divine agency (vidadhāmy aham — I make firm); engineering analog stays at structural-pattern scope. Counter-example explicitly carves out structurally-inappropriate technique-fit so the analog does not collapse into 'commit to anything.'

notes: Cynical-writer test for 7.21: identify the parody-handle. The verse does not give one — the engineering layer is anchored to specific tooling and configurations; the quotable line is testable on the artifact, not on affect. PASS.
